instructions_coverage.notes: All 8 instructions covered: duplicate-check (s1,s2,s3), scope-assessment (s1,s2), as-reported marking (s1,s2), template usage (s1,s2), no-auto-vendor (s1,s3), REVIEWED.md entry with scope-fit (s1,s2,s3), no-auto-promotion (s1,s2,s3), REFERENCE_INDEX update (s1)

scenarios.capability: Triage a session-lifecycle context tool from a GitHub URL into a complete reference summary with scope assessment, REVIEWED.md entry, and REFERENCE_INDEX.md update
scenarios.reason: Agent can fetch the tool's README, derive a slug, assess scope fit, fill the REFERENCE-tool template, update REVIEWED.md and REFERENCE_INDEX.md, and offer the user a promotion/vendor/keep/skip choice — all without running git submodule add or creating an ANALYSIS file.

scenarios.capability: Correctly classify a borderline tool (memory system with a context-layer injection component) as borderline rather than in scope or out of scope
scenarios.reason: Agent can read the tool's README, identify that its primary function is long-term memory storage but it has a context-injection layer, classify it as borderline, document the reasoning in both the reference file and REVIEWED.md, and still produce a useful summary.

scenarios.capability: Detect a tool already present in REVIEWED.md and halt without creating any new files
scenarios.reason: Agent can grep REVIEWED.md and REFERENCE_INDEX.md for the repo URL or slug, identify the existing entry, report it to the user, and stop.
